$comment: Normatively defined by the AIP Principal Token rules. Principal Tokens are JWTs — they use standard JWT header signing, not a custom canonicalization order. The JWT signing covers the compact base64url(header).base64url(payload) string. The JWT header is not validated by this payload schema, but MUST contain typ='JWT', alg='EdDSA', and kid=<issuer-controlled Ed25519 verification method DID URL>. Delegated Principal Tokens issued by parent agent AIDs resolve kid through the authoritative AIP Registry historical public-key endpoint. Delegation rules D-1 through D-5 govern chain validation.
title: AIP Principal Token
description: A signed JWT payload encoding one delegation link in the AIP principal chain. These tokens are embedded as compact-serialised JWTs in the aip_chain array of a Credential Token or Step Execution Token — they are not transmitted as standalone objects. Each element in aip_chain is a complete JWT (header.payload.signature) whose payload conforms to this schema. The JWT header MUST use typ='JWT', alg='EdDSA', and a kid DID URL resolving to an Ed25519 verification method controlled by the issuer. For delegated Principal Tokens issued by parent agent AIDs, the issuer AID MUST be registered and the signing key is resolved through the AIP Registry historical public-key endpoint.

iss.description: Issuer identifier — the DID of the party that issued and signed this Principal Token. For root tokens (delegation_depth=0) this MUST equal principal.id (the root principal's DID). For delegated tokens (delegation_depth>0) this MUST equal the AID of the delegating agent (i.e., the sub of the prior chain element). Added in AIP spec v0.3 (Appendix B). MUST NOT use the did:aip method when delegation_depth=0.

sub.description: AID of the agent this token authorises. The agent identified here is the one being granted the scopes listed in this token.

principal.description: The root principal — the human or organisation ultimately responsible for this agent's actions. This object is carried unchanged through every link in the delegation chain.

type.description: Whether the root principal is a human individual or an organisational entity.

id.description: W3C DID of the ROOT principal — the human or organisation at the top of the entire delegation chain. CRITICAL: This field MUST be byte-for-byte identical across every Principal Token in the same aip_chain array. It always identifies the originating human or organisation — never an intermediate delegating agent. Relying parties MUST reject chains where this value differs between any two elements. MUST NOT use the did:aip method (root principals are humans or organisations, never AIP agents).

oneOf.description: AID of the parent agent that issued this delegation. REQUIRED when delegation_depth > 0. Validation Algorithm Step 8e verifies that this value equals the sub field of the previous chain element (aip_chain[i-1].sub) and does not equal this token's sub value.
MUST be null when delegation_depth is 0 — this token was issued directly by the root principal, with no intermediate agent.

delegated_by.description: AID of the parent agent that issued this delegation. Null when the root principal issued directly. The chain linkage rule: delegated_by in token at index i MUST equal sub in token at index i-1 and MUST NOT equal sub in token i.

delegation_depth.description: Position of this token in the delegation chain. MUST equal the array index of this token in aip_chain (0 for root, 1 for first sub-delegation, up to hard maximum 10). Enforced by Validation Algorithm Rule D-4.

max_delegation_depth.description: OPTIONAL. Maximum total delegation depth permitted from this chain's root. Set by the root principal in aip_chain[0] and MUST NOT be increased by any agent in the chain (Rule D-2). A value of 0 means the authorised agent is a leaf — it cannot spawn sub-agents. Default when absent from aip_chain[0]: 3. Hard maximum: 10. Only the value from aip_chain[0] governs the entire chain — values in subsequent tokens are informational only.

issued_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC datetime string when this delegation was issued (e.g., 2026-03-22T10:00:00Z). For root Principal Tokens produced by AIP-GRANT, this is the issuer's reliable current UTC time at signing and equals the GrantResponse signed_at instant. Note: Principal Token timestamps are ISO 8601 strings; Credential Token timestamps (iat/exp) are Unix integers. When comparing during chain validation, parse ISO 8601 to Unix timestamp. Clock skew tolerance: 30 seconds.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC datetime string after which this delegation is invalid. MUST be strictly after issued_at. For root Principal Tokens produced by AIP-GRANT, this MUST equal issued_at plus approved_delegation_valid_for_seconds from the GrantResponse. Expired delegations are rejected by Validation Algorithm Step 8h with chain_token_expired; future issued_at or non-positive duration is rejected with delegation_chain_invalid.

purpose.description: OPTIONAL. Human-readable description of this agent's intended purpose. Signed consent/audit metadata only. Relying Parties MAY display or log it, but MUST NOT use it to grant capabilities, satisfy scope checks, or override Capability Manifest, Capability Overlay, Approval Envelope, or policy evaluation.

oneOf.description: Unique identifier for the specific task this delegation authorises.
Null when this delegation is not associated with a specific task.

task_id.description: OPTIONAL unless the synced AIP Namespace Catalog entry for the token subject namespace has requires_task_id: true. Links this delegation to a specific task in the audit trail. Validated during Registration Check 11 and during delegation-chain validation Step 8k.

items.description: A scope string in dot-notation format. Each segment begins with a lowercase letter or underscore and may then contain lowercase letters, digits, or underscores. Concrete registered values and metadata come from the synced AIP Scope Catalog.

scope.description: The capability scopes being delegated by this token. MUST be a subset of the delegating party's own Capability Manifest scopes (scope inheritance rule). Duplicate entries are prohibited.

acr.description: OPTIONAL for G1/G2; REQUIRED for G3. Authentication Context Class Reference — the level of identity assurance used when the root principal authenticated to the issuing Principal Wallet. Values follow RFC 8176 / NIST SP 800-63 level conventions (e.g., 'urn:mace:incommon:iap:silver'). Validated by G3 relying parties during grant-tier and identity-proofing checks.

amr.description: OPTIONAL for G1/G2; REQUIRED for G3. Authentication Methods References — array of identifiers for the authentication methods used (per RFC 8176, e.g., ['pwd', 'otp']). REQUIRED when acr is present. Validated by G3 relying parties during grant-tier and identity-proofing checks.

then.description: When delegation_depth >= 1, delegated_by MUST be a non-null did:aip AID.

else.description: When delegation_depth == 0, delegated_by MUST be null (issued directly by the root principal).
